operation.description: The type of action to be performed (get/set)

result.description: The result of the executed action

tool_id.description: The tool responsible for executing the action

total_input_tokens.description: Peak context pressure (root peak + sum of per-agent peaks).

parsed.description: True when a Markdown sidecar was written at upload time.

branches_in_use.description: Branches already checked out by the parent repo or another worktree.

current_branch.description: The checked-out branch, or null when HEAD is detached.

git_repo.description: False (with a `reason`) when the path is missing or not a git repo.

*.description: Partial configuration subtree, deep-merged into the stored configuration. Mirrors the shape served by GET /api/config/schema.

config.description: Configuration values with protected/secret values stripped.

secrets.description: Is-set map for secret fields (never the values themselves).

errors.description: Pydantic validation errors; nothing was saved.

model.description: Optional model override. Any configured LiteLLM model id; a non-string value is ignored and the configured default is used.

query.example: Draft a short release note for the latest deploy.

workspace.description: Wiki slug used for retrieval grounding. Omit for no grounding.

attachments.description: Session attachment display names (session-scoped queries only).

files.description: Git-indexed project files the composer can reference.

git_repo.description: False (with a `reason`) when there is no git project.

label.description: Human-readable label for the key, shown in key listings.

id.description: Stable key id; use it to revoke.

key.description: The plaintext API key — shown ONCE, never retrievable again.

revoked.description: True once the key has been revoked.

keys.description: Metadata for every key; hashes and plaintext are never included.

capabilities.description: Per-model capability map keyed by model name.

models.description: Model names served by the configured LLM proxy.

clear_all.description: When true, clear every notification. Defaults to clearing only dismissed ones.

id.description: Single notification id. Ignored when `ids` is present.

approved.description: True to approve the pending plan, false to reject it.

title.description: Short title for the plan step

marketplace.description: Marketplace the plugin is published in.

name.description: Plugin name as listed by GET /api/plugins/marketplace.

aliases.description: Addressable aliases for the same repository.

available.description: Whether the project path exists on disk.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path to an existing checkout. When omitted, the server provisions a folder for the project.

description.description: New description. Omit to keep the current one.

name.description: New display name. Omit to keep the current one.

error.description: Redacted `{code, message}` on failure; never a secret.

jobs.description: The source's map jobs, newest first.

message.example: the run could not be started

q.example: Which services call the billing API?

cancelled.description: False when the run had already settled (nothing changed).

model.description: Optional model override for this run, as a LiteLLM model name. Wins over the tier's configured model (`scg.traversal.tier_models`); probes inherit it. Omit to let the tier pick.

project.description: Optional project name that scopes connector configuration.

query.example: Which services call the billing API?

tier.description: Search depth: `fast`, `auto` or `deep`. The tier also picks the model that drives the run. Defaults to the server's configured tier.

workspace_id.description: Workspace to search, returned by POST /api/agentic_search/workspaces.

status.description: `running` for an orchestrated run, `completed` for the echo path.

model.description: Per-run model override, or null for the tier default.

query.example: Which services call the billing API?

example: Which services write to the billing DB?

stats.description: Derived run stats; null until a real settle ran.

query.example: Which services call the billing API?

available.description: False when the configured connector's discovery failed.

desc.example: Issues, pull requests and code search.

source_type.description: SCG descriptor kind a map job uses for this source.

tool_ids.description: The tool ids a run on this source is allowed to call.

unavailable_reason.description: Why an unavailable source is greyed out.

sources.description: The catalog of connectors a workspace can enable.

tiers.description: Tier id → the resolved model preset each tier runs on.

desc.example: Issues, code and docs for the platform team

runs.description: The workspace's recent run records, newest first.

workspaces.description: All saved workspaces (filtered by `q` when given).

name.description: Command name without the leading slash.

attachments.description: Attachment descriptors returned by the attachments upload endpoint.

context.description: Free-form context object persisted with the session. Recognized keys include `project`, `model`, `mcp_tools` (tool allowlist), `skill`, and `fallback_models`.

project.description: Project to bind the session to: a configured project name, or `managed:<project_id>` for a managed project or worktree.

session_tag.description: Optional stable tag for looking the session up later.

compact.description: Set to `true` to compact the forked transcript in the background after the fork.

from_ts.description: Fork point: copy events up to this timestamp. Omit to fork the full transcript.

model.description: Model override recorded on the new session.

tag.description: Optional tag applied to the new session.

forked_at.description: Fork-point timestamp, or null for a full-history fork.

run_id.description: New run id (present only when an idle session was re-engaged).

text.description: Message text. Steers the active run, or re-engages an idle session as a new query.
text.example: Focus on the failing tests first.

attachments.description: Attachment descriptors returned by the attachments upload endpoint.

context.description: Free-form context object persisted with the session. Recognized keys include `project`, `model`, `mcp_tools` (tool allowlist), `skill`, and `fallback_models`.

project.description: Project whose directory the run executes in: a configured project name or `managed:<project_id>`.

query.description: The user message to run, or a slash command such as `/status` or `/terminate`.

skill.description: Name of a skill to activate for this run.

skill_args.description: Arguments passed to the activated skill.

action.description: `retry` re-runs the last user query; `continue` resumes from where the failed run stopped.

edited_text.description: Replacement text for the recovered query.

from_ts.description: Timestamp of the user message to recover from. Defaults to the most recent one.

model.description: Model override for the recovered run.

job_id.description: Wiki-indexing job id (returned instead of `run_id` for indexing).

run_id.description: Generic recovery run id; absent for wiki-indexing recovery.

description.example: Fan-out web research with cited synthesis.

auth_scope.description: Redacted label for the auth the connector carries, for example `oauth:repo`. Never a token or credential.

descriptor.description: The connector's self-description, such as an MCP tool list or an OpenAPI document. Optional for `mcp_tool_list` sources, where it is built from the connector's live tool list when omitted.

model.description: Optional model override for the mapping session, as a LiteLLM model name.

nl_context.description: Optional workspace prose that seeds the mapping step, with `workspace_instructions` and `workspace_description` keys. Usually injected automatically when a workspace is saved.

source_type.description: Kind of connector being mapped, for example `mcp_tool_list`. `text` is not yet supported and returns 422.

reason.example: a structured run is already active for this session
the run could not be started

message.example: structured responses are not configured on this server

mode.description: Execution strategy. Omit (default 'agentic') for the tool-using, session-backed run that returns a run handle to poll. 'synthesis' selects a no-loop, retrieval-only single round-trip (no tools): it returns inline with status 'completed', the validated output, and grounding citations — lower latency for cheap structured extraction.

model.description: Optional model override. Any configured LiteLLM model id; a non-string value is ignored and the configured default is used.

query.example: List the public HTTP endpoints and what each one returns.

schema.description: JSON Schema the output object must validate against.

tools.description: Allowlist of tool ids the run may use. Omit for the default set.

workspace.description: Wiki slug or search workspace that grounds the run. A workspace mapped to an indexed search workspace also grants graph traversal tools.

output.description: The schema-validated result object — present only once the run has completed.

example.summary: The API exposes 4 public HTTP endpoints.

run_id.description: Run handle of the form <session_id>:r<seq>; poll GET /v1/structured/{run_id}.

status.description: 'completed' when the output is attached inline, else 'running'.

workspace.description: The grounding workspace echoed from the request (may be null).

recipes_routed.description: Count of scg_route calls that proposed pathways.

output.description: The schema-validated result object, attached once the run has completed.

example.summary: The API exposes 4 public HTTP endpoints.

provenance.description: Graph-first pathway/probe provenance (additive; absent for plain/wiki runs).

status.description: Run state: 'completed', 'running', or a terminal session status.

attachments.description: Attachment descriptors returned by the attachments upload endpoint.

context.description: Free-form context object persisted with the session. Recognized keys include `project`, `model`, and `mcp_tools` (tool allowlist).

fork_from.description: Session id or tag to fork the new session from.

project.description: Project whose directory the run executes in: a configured project name or `managed:<project_id>`.

query.description: The user query to run to completion.
query.example: What changed in the last release?

session_tag.description: Human-friendly tag resolving to a session (created if new).

task_result.description: Combined response of all action steps

title.description: New display title. Trimmed and capped at 120 characters.

description.example: Run a shell command in the project directory.

message.example: a structured run is already active for this session
the run could not be started

api_url.description: Forge REST API base URL. Required for the final answer to be posted back to the issue or pull request as a comment.

assignee.description: Login the item was assigned to.

bot_login.description: Configured bot login, used to ignore the bot's own comments.

comment.description: Text of the mention comment that triggered the pickup, when present.

head_ref.description: Pull request head branch. When set, the session runs in a worktree checked out on this branch.

kind.description: Whether the trigger is an issue or a pull request.

model.description: Optional model override. Any configured LiteLLM model id; omit for the configured default.

project.description: Project key override. Defaults to the repository.

prompt.description: Full override of the generated agent prompt.

provider.description: Forge that sent the event. Informational.

url.description: Web URL of the issue or pull request.

accepted.description: True when a fresh run was accepted and started.

enqueued.description: True (202 path) when the prompt was steered into an already-active run.

reason.description: Why the trigger was skipped (present only when skipped).

resumed.description: True when an existing session for this item was continued.

run_id.description: Run handle for the started run (absent on a skipped trigger).

session_id.description: The tag-keyed session the run was started or continued on.

skipped.description: True when the trigger was a no-op (e.g. the bot's own comment).

worktree_id.description: Managed worktree the run is bound to, or null for the main checkout.

desc.description: Short description shown in workspace lists.
desc.example: Issues, code and docs for the platform team

instructions.description: Guidance applied to every run in this workspace, such as preferred repositories or terminology.

sources.description: Ids of the sources to enable, from GET /api/agentic_search/sources. Defaults to no sources.

unmapped.description: True on a ghost node for an unmapped source.
Scoped sources that produced zero schema nodes.

sources.description: Replacement list of enabled source ids. Changing the selection can start background mapping of newly enabled sources.

clean.description: True when the worktree has no uncommitted changes.

managed.description: True for API-created worktrees, false for plain-git ones.

project_id.description: The worktree's own managed id (null for unmanaged on-disk worktrees).

status.description: Idempotent marker: the worktree was already gone.

base.description: Optional base ref. When set, a fresh `branch` is created from this ref instead of requiring the branch to exist.

branch.description: Branch to check out in the new worktree. Must already exist unless `base` is provided.

info.description:
Everything the Mewbo web console can do is available over plain HTTP. Create sessions and send queries. Stream events live. Run multi-source searches. Get schema-constrained answers for pipelines. Every endpoint below is generated from the running server, so this reference is always current with the code.

## Base URL

A self-hosted stack serves the API on port `5125` by default:

```
http://localhost:5125
```

Replace the host with wherever your stack runs. The request samples on this page use this base URL.

## Authentication

Every endpoint expects an API key in the `X-API-KEY` header:

```bash
curl -H "X-API-KEY: $MEWBO_API_KEY" http://localhost:5125/api/models
```

Two kinds of keys work. The master token from `configs/app.json` (`api.master_token`) always works, and it is the only key allowed to mint others via `POST /api/keys`. Minted keys work everywhere else and can be revoked individually, so give each integration its own.

Browsers cannot set headers on `EventSource` connections. Server-sent event endpoints therefore also accept the key as a query parameter: `?api_key=<key>`.

## Make your first request

Three calls take you from nothing to a live agent run:

```bash
# 1. Create a session
curl -X POST http://localhost:5125/api/sessions \
  -H "X-API-KEY: $MEWBO_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}'
# -> {"session_id": "9e2d47c1..."}

# 2. Send it a query
curl -X POST http://localhost:5125/api/sessions/9e2d47c1.../query \
  -H "X-API-KEY: $MEWBO_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"query": "Summarize the open pull requests."}'

# 3. Watch the run live
curl -N "http://localhost:5125/api/sessions/9e2d47c1.../stream?api_key=$MEWBO_API_KEY"
```

The stream closes when the run finishes. The full transcript stays available through `GET /api/sessions/{session_id}/events`.

The session lifecycle is four steps, all keyed by the same `session_id`:

1. **Create** — `POST /api/sessions` returns a `session_id`.
2. **Query** — `POST /api/sessions/{session_id}/query` starts a run.
3. **Watch** — `GET /api/sessions/{session_id}/stream` pushes events live    over SSE until the run reaches a terminal state.
4. **Replay** — `GET /api/sessions/{session_id}/events` returns the full    transcript at any time afterwards.

## Long-running work

Searches, structured runs, and indexing jobs can outlive a single request. These endpoints return a run or job id right away, then settle into one terminal status — `completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`. The pattern is the same for all of them:

1. **Start** — call the create endpoint; it returns a run or job id    immediately. Structured run handles have the form `<session_id>:r<seq>`,    so the part before the first colon is always a session you can stream.
2. **Follow** — either poll the matching `GET .../{run_id}` endpoint, or    subscribe to its `GET .../{run_id}/events` SSE stream.
3. **Read** — once the status is terminal, the poll stops and the stream    closes; the run carries its result.

For Agentic Search specifically, pick a workspace with `GET /api/agentic_search/workspaces`, start a run with `POST /api/agentic_search/runs`, follow it with the steps above, and read the cited answer with its sources off the terminal run.

## Streaming

Endpoints ending in `/stream` or `/events` speak [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events). Connect once and keep reading. Events are pushed the moment they happen; there is no polling interval to tune.

## Errors

Failures return a JSON body, never an HTML page. Expect `{"message": "..."}` on auth and validation errors, and `{"error": {"code", "reason", "retryable"}}` from the structured and search surfaces. Unknown routes return the same JSON envelope with a 404.

## Related guides

The [Web Console + API](../clients-web-api/) guide covers concepts like capability negotiation and sharing. [Building a Client](../developer-guide/) walks through a minimal integration. The Agentic Wiki has its own HTTP surface under `/v1/wiki/*`, documented in the [Agentic Wiki](../features-wiki/) guide.
info.title: Mewbo API

post.description:
Starts a search run over a workspace's enabled sources. POST `{workspace_id, query}` (optionally `tier` and a per-run `model` override). The response always carries `run_id`, `session_id`, `status` and the full `run` snapshot. An orchestrated run returns `running` promptly — then poll `GET /runs/{run_id}` or attach to `GET /runs/{run_id}/events` (SSE) to follow it; the echo path settles synchronously as `completed`.
Runs the search agent over the workspace's enabled sources. The
response always carries `run_id`, `session_id`, `status` and the full
`run` snapshot. An orchestrated run returns `running` promptly and
settles through the event stream or by polling `GET /runs/{run_id}`;
the echo path settles synchronously as `completed`. Set `tier` to
trade depth for latency; the tier also picks the model that drives
the run.

200.description: Run started; body carries the run snapshot plus its ids.

400.description: Malformed, missing, or invalid request body or parameters.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

get.description:
Returns the durable run snapshot — status, query, tier, timestamps and the results/answer accumulated so far. Safe to poll while a run is `running`, and self-sufficient for reload, share and deep-link views (`/search?ws=<id>&run=<id>`) with no other context. Any valid API key resolves the same run by id.
Returns the durable run snapshot: status, query, tier, timestamps and
the results and answer accumulated so far. Safe to poll while a run
is `running`, and self-sufficient for reload, share and deep-link
views with no other context.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

post.description:
Requests cancellation of an in-flight run and, best effort, the session backing it. The `cancelled` flag is false when the run had already settled (in which case nothing changes). The terminal state still arrives on the event stream and the snapshot.
Requests cancellation of an in-flight run and, best effort, the
session backing it. The `cancelled` flag in the response is false
when the run had already settled, in which case nothing changes.
The terminal state still arrives on the event stream and snapshot.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

get.description:
Server-sent events (`text/event-stream`): replays the run's append-only event log from the start, then tails it live until a terminal event. Attach an `EventSource` to this URL right after `POST /runs` to render a live result reveal. Each frame's `id:` line is the event index, so a dropped connection resumes via `?after_idx=` or the `Last-Event-ID` header. EventSource can't set headers, so the API key is also accepted as the `?api_key=` query parameter.
Server-sent events (`text/event-stream`): replays the run's
append-only event log from the start, then tails it live until a
terminal event. Each frame's `id` line carries the event index, so a
dropped connection resumes with `after_idx` or the `Last-Event-ID`
header. Because EventSource cannot set headers, the API key is also
accepted as the `api_key` query parameter.

parameters.description: Replay only events with an index greater than this value. Defaults to -1, a full replay from the start.
parameters.name: after_idx

parameters.description: API key, for EventSource clients that cannot set the X-API-Key header.
parameters.name: api_key

200.description: Server-sent event stream of run events.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

get.description:
Returns node/edge/source/recipe counts for the Source Capability Graph plus the list of mapped sources — use it to confirm that map jobs have populated the graph before relying on orchestrated runs. A deterministic read that never invokes a model. Requires `scg.enabled` (503 when off).
Returns node, edge, source and recipe counts for the Source
Capability Graph, plus the list of mapped sources with their types.
Useful to confirm that map jobs have populated the graph. The read is
deterministic and never invokes a model. Returns 503 when
`scg.enabled` is off.

200.description: Graph counts and the mapped source list.

get.description:
Call this first when building a workspace: each entry's `id` is what you pass in a workspace's `sources` array. A configured source whose discovery failed stays listed with `available: false`. Pass `?project=` to scope the catalog to one project's connector configuration.
Returns the catalog of connectors a workspace can enable. Each entry
describes one source: its id, display name, type and availability.
A configured source whose discovery failed stays listed with
`available` false rather than being omitted. Pass `project` to scope
the catalog to one project's configuration.

parameters.description: Project name that scopes the catalog to that project's connector configuration.
parameters.name: project

post.description:
Starts a background job that indexes the connector's schema into the Source Capability Graph, making the source routable by search runs. The response carries a `job_id` — poll `GET /sources/{source_id}/map/jobs/{job_id}` or follow `GET /sources/{source_id}/map/events` (SSE). For an `mcp_tool_list` source you can omit `descriptor` and one is built from the connector's live tool list. Requires `scg.enabled` (503 when off).
Starts a background job that indexes the connector's schema into the
Source Capability Graph, which makes the source routable by search
runs. The job is asynchronous: the response carries a `job_id` to
poll via `GET /sources/{source_id}/map/jobs/{job_id}` or to follow on
the map event stream. When `descriptor` is omitted for an
`mcp_tool_list` source, one is built from the connector's live tool
list; a source with no configured connector returns 422 instead.
Returns 503 when `scg.enabled` is off.

parameters.description: Project name used to locate the connector when building a descriptor from its live tool list.
parameters.name: project

202.description: Map job accepted; track it via the job and event endpoints.

400.description: Malformed, missing, or invalid request body or parameters.

422.description: Unsupported source type, or no connector available to introspect.

503.description: Source Capability Graph is disabled, or the mapper is unavailable.

get.description:
Server-sent events (`text/event-stream`) over a mapping job's append-only event log: replays from the start, then tails live until a terminal event. The newest job for the source streams by default; pass `?job_id=` to pick one. A dropped connection resumes via `?after_idx=` or `Last-Event-ID`. EventSource can't set headers, so the API key is also accepted as `?api_key=`.
Server-sent events (`text/event-stream`) over a mapping job's
append-only event log: replays from the start, then tails live until
a terminal event. The newest job for the source is streamed by
default; pass `job_id` to pick one. A dropped connection resumes with
`after_idx` or the `Last-Event-ID` header. Because EventSource cannot
set headers, the API key is also accepted as the `api_key` query
parameter.

parameters.description: Map job to stream. Defaults to the newest job for the source.
parameters.name: job_id

parameters.description: Replay only events with an index greater than this value. Defaults to -1, a full replay from the start.
parameters.name: after_idx

parameters.description: API key, for EventSource clients that cannot set the X-API-Key header.
parameters.name: api_key

200.description: Server-sent event stream of map job events.

404.description: No map job for the source, or unknown job id.

get.description:
Returns the source's mapping jobs, newest first. Each carries a coarse `status` (`queued`/`running`/`completed`/`failed`) and a fine-grained `phase`. Poll this after `POST /sources/{source_id}/map` to follow progress without holding an SSE stream open.
Returns the source's mapping jobs, newest first. Each record carries
the job's status (`queued`, `running`, `completed` or `failed`) and
its progress phase. Poll this after starting a map job to follow it
without holding an event stream open.

200.description: Map jobs for the source, newest first.

get.summary: List map jobs for a source

get.description:
Returns one mapping job's record. The job must belong to the source in the path, else 404. Poll this until `status` settles to `completed` or `failed`.
Returns one mapping job's record. The job must belong to the source
in the path; otherwise the response is 404. Poll this until the
status settles to `completed` or `failed`.

parameters.description: Map job id returned by POST /sources/{source_id}/map.
parameters.name: job_id

404.description: Map job not found, or it belongs to another source.

get.description:
Use this to populate a tier picker before a run: the response maps each tier id to the model it will actually drive with, so a client can show `auto · claude-haiku-4-5` before submit. A pure config read — available even when `scg.enabled` is off.
A tier is the run's one budget knob — decomposition depth and probe
fan-out — and it also presets the model that drives the run
(`scg.traversal.tier_models`). A blank mapping resolves to
`llm.default_model`, mirroring exactly what the drive does, so the
returned model is the one that actually runs unless the request
carries an explicit `model` override. Pure config read — available
regardless of `scg.enabled`.

200.description: Tier ids mapped to the model each tier runs on.

get.summary: List the search tiers and their model presets

get.description:
Lists every saved workspace with its enabled sources, instructions and recent query history. Pass `?q=` to filter (case-insensitive substring) across name, description and past-query text — handy for a workspace switcher's search box.
Returns all saved workspaces, each with its enabled sources,
instructions and recent query history. Pass `q` to filter by name,
description or past-query text.

parameters.description: Case-insensitive filter matched against workspace name, description and past-query text.
parameters.name: q

post.description:
Creates a workspace from a name plus a selection of source ids (from `GET /sources`) and optional run instructions. The new workspace is returned with its generated `id` — use that id to start runs. Creating one may kick off background mapping of newly enabled live sources.
A workspace names a set of enabled sources plus optional run
instructions. Creating one also refreshes its connector configuration
and may start mapping newly enabled live sources in the background.
The new workspace is returned with its generated `id`.

parameters.description: Project name used when auto-mapping newly enabled sources.
parameters.name: project

400.description: Malformed, missing, or invalid request body or parameters.

delete.description:
Removes the workspace and its stored connector configuration (including any auth material that configuration carried). Past runs stay readable by id. The response echoes the deleted id with `deleted: true`.
Removes the workspace and its stored connector configuration,
including any auth material that configuration carried. Past runs
remain readable by id. The response confirms the deleted id.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

patch.description:
Applies a partial update — only `name`, `desc`, `sources` and `instructions` are writable, and omitted fields keep their current values. Send just the fields you want to change. Changing the source selection or instructions can trigger background re-mapping. Returns the full updated workspace.
Applies a partial update: only `name`, `desc`, `sources` and
`instructions` are writable, and omitted fields keep their current
values. Changing the source selection or the instructions can start
background re-mapping of the affected sources. Returns the full
updated workspace.

parameters.description: Project name used when auto-mapping newly enabled sources.
parameters.name: project

400.description: Malformed, missing, or invalid request body or parameters.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

get.description:
Returns the capability graph scoped to the workspace's enabled sources as cytoscape-style `nodes` and `edges` plus `stats` and the resolved `scope`. Render it directly with a cytoscape-compatible viewer; every element carries a `layer` (`schema`/`memory`/`entity`/`cross`) so clients can toggle layers, and a never-mapped source appears as one ghost node flagged `unmapped`. Degrades gracefully — a disabled or unavailable graph backend still returns 200 with an empty schema layer and every source listed unmapped; only an unknown workspace 404s. No node or edge ever carries a credential.
Returns the capability graph scoped to the workspace's enabled
sources, as cytoscape-style `nodes` and `edges` plus `stats` and the
resolved `scope`. Every element is tagged with a layer (`schema`,
`memory`, `entity` or `cross`) so clients can toggle layers
independently. A source that has never been mapped appears as one
ghost node flagged `unmapped`. The endpoint degrades gracefully: a
disabled or unavailable graph backend still returns 200 with an empty
schema layer and every source listed as unmapped. Only an unknown
workspace returns 404. No node or edge ever carries a credential.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

get.description:
Returns the resolved `scope` and the aggregate `stats` (node/edge counts, per-kind and per-layer tallies, and the unmapped-source list) — the same figures the full graph carries, minus the node/edge arrays. Use this for a landing health band so it never downloads the full graph. Degrades and 404s identically to the full graph route.
Returns the resolved `scope` and the aggregate `stats` (node/edge
counts, per-kind and per-layer tallies, and the unmapped-source list)
— the same figures the full graph carries, without the node/edge
arrays. Degrades and 404s identically to the full graph route.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

get.description:
Returns the workspace's recent run records, newest first — use it to rebuild a run-history list in a client. Each record is a durable snapshot; fetch one run with `GET /runs/{run_id}` for the full payload. An unknown workspace id yields an empty list (not a 404).
Returns the workspace's recent run records, newest first. Use it to
rebuild run history in a client; fetch one run with
`GET /runs/{run_id}` for the full snapshot. An unknown workspace id
yields an empty list.

post.description:
Start or continue an agent session for an issue or pull-request event from CI (GitHub / Gitea Actions). Sessions are keyed by the deterministic tag `vcs:<owner/repo>:<kind>:<number>`, so repeated triggers for the same item reuse one conversation.

**Outcomes:**

- `200` — a fresh run was started (`accepted: true`, with `run_id`), or the trigger was a no-op (`skipped: true`, e.g. the bot's own comment).
- `202` — a run was already active, so the prompt was enqueued as a steering message (`enqueued: true`).

Pull-request pickups with a `head_ref` run in a worktree checked out on that branch; issue pickups get an isolated `mewbo/issue-<n>` worktree. When a forge token is configured server-side (`channels.vcs.tokens`), the agent's final answer is posted back to the issue/PR as a comment.
Starts or continues an agent session for an issue or pull request event
from CI. Sessions are keyed by the deterministic tag
`vcs:<owner/repo>:<kind>:<number>`, so repeated triggers for the same item
reuse one conversation. If a run is already active on that session, the
new prompt is enqueued as a steering message and the request returns 202.
Pull request pickups with a `head_ref` run in a worktree checked out on
that branch. When a forge token is configured server-side, the final
answer is posted back to the issue or pull request as a comment.

200.description: Run started, or trigger skipped (e.g. the bot's own comment)

202.description: Prompt enqueued as a steering message into the already-active run

400.description: Malformed, missing, or invalid request body or parameters.

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

409.description: The resource is in a conflicting state for this operation.

422.description: Understood but unprocessable — the request could not be carried out.

get.description:
Return the server-side slash command registry — each command's `name`, `args`, and render `kind` — so clients can build command palettes without hardcoding the list. Execute one against a session via POST /api/sessions/{session_id}/command.
Returns the server-side slash command registry with each command's
name, arguments, and render kind, so clients can build command
palettes without hardcoding the list.

Return the current configuration with protected and secret values stripped, plus a `secrets` map reporting which secret fields are set (true/false) without revealing their values. Update with PATCH on the same path.
Returns the current configuration with protected and secret values
stripped, plus a `secrets` map reporting which secret fields are set
(true or false) without revealing their values.

200.description: Configuration values and secret status map.

patch.description:
Deep-merge the request body into the stored configuration, validate the result, and persist it. Attempts to modify protected fields are rejected with 403. A merge that fails validation returns 422 with the validation errors and changes nothing. Mirrors the shape served by GET /api/config/schema.
Deep-merges the request body into the stored configuration, validates
the result, and persists it. Attempts to modify protected fields are
rejected with 403. A merge that fails validation returns 422 with the
validation errors and changes nothing.

200.description: Updated configuration values and secret status map.

400.description: The request body was empty.

403.description: The patch touched a protected field.

422.description: Merged configuration failed validation; nothing was saved.

get.description:
Return the JSON Schema describing the application configuration. Protected fields are stripped entirely and secret fields are marked `writeOnly`, so the schema can drive a settings UI directly. The current values are served separately by GET /api/config.
Returns the JSON Schema describing the application configuration.
Protected fields are stripped entirely and secret fields are marked
`writeOnly`, so the schema can drive a settings UI directly.

List the files an `@<ref>` may resolve to: the project's git index (tracked + new-but-not-`.gitignore`d) plus any files attached to the session. Pass `project` (home composer) or `session` (in-session composer). Falls back to a bounded filesystem walk for non-git projects, and returns empty lists when nothing resolves.
Returns the files an `@<ref>` may resolve to: the project's git index
(tracked + new-but-not-`.gitignore`d) plus any files attached to the
session. Pass `project` (home composer) or `session` (in-session
composer). Falls back to a bounded filesystem walk for non-git
projects.

parameters.description: Project name to scope files to (home composer).
parameters.name: project

parameters.description: Session id to scope files to (in-session composer); also includes the session's attachments.
parameters.name: session

200.description: The project's git-indexed files plus session attachments.

404.description: A `session` was supplied but no session with that id exists.

get.description:
List metadata for every API key — id, label, creation time, and revocation state. Hashes and plaintext values are never returned. Requires the **master** token. Use a key's `id` with `DELETE /api/keys/{key_id}` to revoke it.
Returns metadata for every key: id, label, creation time, and
revocation state. Hashes and plaintext key values are never included.
Requires the master token.

post.description:
Mint a new API key for the `X-API-Key` header. The plaintext key is returned exactly once in the `key` field — store it immediately, it cannot be retrieved again. Requires the **master** token; keys minted here cannot manage other keys.

`curl -XPOST -H 'X-API-Key: <master>' -d '{"label":"ci-deploy"}' <base>/api/keys`
Creates a new API key for use in the `X-API-Key` header. The plaintext
key is returned exactly once in this response and cannot be retrieved
again, so store it securely. Requires the master token; keys minted
here cannot manage other keys.

201.description: Key created. The plaintext key is in the response body.

400.description: The `label` field is missing or empty.

delete.description:
Permanently revoke a key by its `id`. Any request presenting a revoked key is rejected with 401 from that point on. Requires the **master** token. Revocation is irreversible — mint a new key to replace it.
Permanently revokes the key. Requests presenting a revoked key are
rejected with 401 from that point on. Requires the master token.

404.description: No key with that id exists.

get.description:
List the model names served by the configured LLM proxy, the default model, and a per-model capability map. Read `capabilities[name].supports_vision` to decide whether image attachments can be sent to a given model before uploading them.
Returns the model names served by the configured LLM proxy, the
default model, and a per-model capability map. Use
`capabilities[name].supports_vision` to decide whether image
attachments can be sent to a given model.

200.description: Model names, default model, and capability map.

get.description:
List session lifecycle notifications (created, completed, failed). Dismissed entries are hidden unless `include_dismissed=true`. Dismiss with POST /api/notifications/dismiss.
Returns session lifecycle notifications such as session created,
completed, or failed. Dismissed entries are hidden unless
`include_dismissed=true`.

parameters.description: Set to true to include dismissed notifications.
parameters.name: include_dismissed

post.description:
Delete dismissed notifications, or every notification when `clear_all` is true. Returns the number cleared.
Deletes dismissed notifications, or every notification when
`clear_all` is true. Returns the number cleared.

Mark the given notification ids as dismissed. Accepts either an `ids` array or a single `id`. Returns the number dismissed.
Marks the given notification ids as dismissed. Accepts either an
`ids` array or a single `id`. Returns the number dismissed.

get.description:
List each installed plugin with its version, source marketplace, scope, and component counts (skills, agents, commands, MCP servers, hooks). Browse installable plugins via GET /api/plugins/marketplace.
Returns each installed plugin with its version, source marketplace,
scope, and component counts: skills, agents, commands, MCP servers,
and hooks.

List the plugins available for installation from the configured marketplaces. Install one with POST on this same path.
Returns the plugins available for installation from the configured
marketplaces. Install one with POST on this same path.

post.description:
Install the named plugin from a configured marketplace. Its skills, commands, agents, and MCP servers become available to sessions started after installation. Both `name` and `marketplace` are required (from GET /api/plugins/marketplace).
Installs the named plugin from a configured marketplace. Its skills,
commands, agents, and MCP servers become available to sessions started
after installation.

400.description: Missing fields or unknown plugin/marketplace.

delete.description:
Remove an installed plugin and its components from the install directory. Sessions started afterward no longer see its skills, commands, agents, or MCP servers.
Removes an installed plugin and its components from the install
directory.

parameters.description: Name of an installed plugin, as listed by GET /api/plugins.
parameters.name: plugin_name

404.description: Plugin not found.

get.description:
List configuration-defined and managed projects in one array. Each entry carries an `available` flag (does its path exist on disk) and, for git checkouts, a `repo` identity plus `aliases` (e.g. `owner/repo`) that address the same project elsewhere in the API. Managed worktrees appear as child entries with `is_worktree` set.
Returns configuration-defined and managed projects in one list. Each
entry carries an `available` flag (whether its path exists on disk)
and, for git checkouts, a `repo` identity plus `aliases` such as
`owner/repo` that address the same project elsewhere in the API.
Managed worktrees appear as child entries with `is_worktree` set.

post.description:
Run the query to completion and return the full result in one response, including the executed action steps and the `session_id`. Prefer the asynchronous session endpoints for interactive use; this remains for simple CLI-style clients. A new session is created automatically unless `session_id`, `session_tag`, or `fork_from` selects an existing one.
Runs the query to completion and returns the full result in one
response, including the executed action steps. POST /api/query remains
supported as a simple synchronous alternative for CLI-style clients;
prefer the asynchronous session endpoints for interactive use. A new
session is created automatically unless `session_id`, `session_tag`,
or `fork_from` selects an existing one.

200.description: Completed run with the executed action steps.

400.description: The `query` field is missing, or the named project is invalid.

get.description:
List one summary per session — status, title, timestamps, and an `origin` (`user`, `wiki`, `search`, or `channel`) describing what created it. Archived sessions are hidden unless `include_archived=true`.
Returns one summary per session with status, title, timestamps, and an
`origin` field (`user`, `wiki`, `search`, or `channel`) describing
what created it. Archived sessions are hidden unless
`include_archived=true`.

parameters.description: Set to true to include archived sessions.
parameters.name: include_archived

post.description:
Create an empty session and return its `session_id`. Optionally bind a project, apply a lookup `session_tag`, and persist initial context (e.g. the model). Clients may declare capabilities via the `X-Mewbo-Capabilities` header (comma separated). Run queries with POST /api/sessions/{session_id}/query. An explicit `cwd` requires `api.allow_external_cwd`.
Creates an empty session and returns its `session_id`. Optionally
binds a project, applies a lookup tag, and persists initial context
such as the model to use. Clients may declare capabilities via the
`X-Mewbo-Capabilities` header (comma separated). Run queries against
the session with POST /api/sessions/{session_id}/query.

200.description: Session created; body carries the new `session_id`.

400.description: An explicit `cwd` was supplied but the path does not exist / is not a dir.

403.description: An explicit `cwd` was supplied but `api.allow_external_cwd` is disabled.

get.description:
Return the session's sub-agent lifecycle events (status, model, per-agent token counts) plus rollups: `total_steps`, `total_input_tokens` (peak context pressure), and `total_input_tokens_billed` (cumulative billed input). Use it to render a live agent tree alongside the event stream.
Returns the session's sub-agent lifecycle events with status, model,
and per-agent token counts, plus rollups: `total_steps`,
`total_input_tokens` (peak context pressure), and
`total_input_tokens_billed` (cumulative billed input). Use it to
render a live agent tree alongside the event stream.

delete.description:
Restore an archived session to the default session list.
Restores an archived session to the default session list.

404.description: No session with that id exists.

post.description:
Hide the session from the default session list. Archiving is fully reversible with DELETE on the same path.
Hides the session from the default session list. Archiving is fully
reversible with DELETE on the same path.

404.description: No session with that id exists.

post.description:
Upload one or more files as `multipart/form-data` under the `files` field (a single `file` field also works). Documents are parsed to Markdown at upload time so later runs read them without re-parsing. Unsupported file types are rejected, as are image uploads when the `model` hint names a non-vision model. Reference the returned descriptors in the `attachments` field of a query.
Accepts one or more files as `multipart/form-data` under the `files`
field (a single `file` field also works). Documents are parsed to
Markdown at upload time so later runs can read them without
re-parsing. Unsupported file types are rejected, as are image uploads
when the `model` hint names a model without vision support. Reference
the returned descriptors in the `attachments` field of a query.

parameters.description: Optional model hint. Image uploads are rejected early when the named model lacks vision support.
parameters.name: model

400.description: No files, an unsupported type, or an image to a non-vision model.

404.description: No session with that id exists.

post.description:
Execute a server-side slash command (e.g. `compact`) against the session. Commands that render into the transcript run asynchronously like a query: the call returns 202 and output arrives on the event stream. Dialog and notification commands execute inline and return their result with 200. Discover commands via GET /api/commands.
Executes a server-side slash command such as `compact` against the
session. Commands that render into the transcript run asynchronously
like a regular query: the call returns 202 and their output arrives on
the event stream. Dialog and notification commands execute inline and
return their result in the response body with 200. Discover available
commands via GET /api/commands.

200.description: Inline command result (dialog or notification render).

202.description: Transcript command started; watch the event stream.

400.description: Missing name or invalid arguments.

404.description: Unknown command.

get.description:
Return the session's event timeline plus authoritative run state — `running`, `status`, `done_reason`, `title`, and `recoverable`. Pass `after` (the `ts` of your last event) to fetch only newer events while polling; the status fields are always computed from the full transcript. Prefer the stream endpoint for push delivery.
Returns the session's event timeline plus authoritative run state:
`running`, `status`, `done_reason`, `title`, and `recoverable`. Pass
`after` to fetch only new events while polling; the status fields are
always computed from the full transcript. Prefer the stream endpoint
when you want push delivery.

parameters.description: Return only events with a timestamp strictly after this value. Use the `ts` of the last event you received.
parameters.name: after

parameters.description: Set to 1 or true to cap large free-text payload fields (results, tool inputs, errors) at 2000 characters.
parameters.name: truncate

404.description: No session with that id exists.

get.description:
Return the full event transcript and the stored summary in one payload, suitable for download or offline analysis.
Returns the full event transcript and the stored summary in one
payload, suitable for download or offline analysis.

404.description: No session with that id exists.

post.description:
Copy the transcript into a new session and return its id. Pass `from_ts` to fork from a specific point instead of the full history, and optionally apply a new `tag` or `model`. Set `compact: true` to compact the forked transcript in the background. A running session cannot be forked.
Copies the transcript into a new session and returns its id. Pass
`from_ts` to fork from a specific point instead of the full history.
The fork records its provenance and can apply a new tag or model. A
running session cannot be forked.

201.description: Fork created; body carries the new `session_id`.

400.description: The fork failed (for example, an unknown `from_ts` fork point).

409.description: Cannot fork a session while it is running.

get.description:
Return a unified git diff for the session's bound project. `scope` selects `uncommitted` (working tree vs HEAD, the default) or `branch` (vs the merge base with origin/main or origin/master). When the session has no project, or it is not a git repo, the call still returns 200 with `git_repo` false and a `reason`.
Returns a unified git diff for the session's bound project. When the
session has no project, or the project is not a git repository, the
call still returns 200 with `git_repo` false and a `reason` instead of
an error.

parameters.description: `uncommitted` (default) diffs the working tree against HEAD; `branch` diffs against the merge base with origin/main (or origin/master).
parameters.name: scope

200.description: Unified diff, or `git_repo: false` with a reason.

400.description: `scope` must be `uncommitted` or `branch`.

404.description: No session with that id exists.

post.description:
Stop the currently executing step of an active run and return 202. Interrupting an idle session is an idempotent no-op that returns 200 with `interrupted` false, so the call is always safe to make.
Stops the currently executing step of an active run and returns 202.
Interrupting an idle session is an idempotent no-op that returns 200
with `interrupted` false, so the call is always safe to make.

200.description: Session was idle; nothing to interrupt (`interrupted: false`).

post.description:
While a run is active, the `text` is enqueued as a steering message and the call returns 202. On an idle or finished session the message re-engages it: a fresh run starts with `text` as its query and the call returns 200 with the new `run_id` (form `<session_id>:r<seq>`). Only a terminated session rejects.
While a run is active the text is enqueued as a steering message for
the agent and the call returns 202. On an idle or finished session the
message re-engages it instead: a fresh run starts with the text as its
query and the call returns 200 with the new `run_id`. Run ids have the
form `<session_id>:r<seq>`. Only a terminated session rejects.

200.description: Idle session re-engaged; body carries the new `run_id`.

202.description: Steering message enqueued into the active run.

400.description: The `text` field is missing or empty.

409.description: The session could not be re-engaged.

get.description:
Return the session's current `plan.md` as `text/markdown`. A plan file exists only after a plan-mode run has written one; otherwise the call returns 404.
Returns the session's current `plan.md` as `text/markdown`. A plan
file exists only after a plan-mode run has written one; otherwise the
call returns 404.

400.description: The session id is invalid (path traversal guard).

404.description: No plan file exists for this session.

500.description: The plan file could not be read.

post.description:
Resolve a pending plan-mode proposal. Approval (`approved: true`) immediately starts a new act-mode run that implements the plan; rejection leaves the session dormant so the user can send refinement guidance via the query endpoint. Returns 404 when no proposal is pending or a run is already active.
Resolves a pending plan-mode proposal. Approval immediately starts a
new run in act mode that implements the approved plan; rejection
leaves the session dormant so the user can send refinement guidance
via the query endpoint. Returns 404 when no proposal is pending.

400.description: `approved` must be a boolean.

404.description: No pending plan proposal, or a run is already active.

500.description: The plan was approved but the follow-up run could not start.

post.description:
Start an asynchronous run for `query` and return 202 immediately; follow progress via the events or stream endpoints. The slash commands `/terminate` and `/status` are handled inline without starting a run (`/status` returns 200 with the session state). A session runs one turn at a time, so a second call while one is active returns 409. `@file`/`@dir`/`@diff`/`@url` references in the query are expanded before the run.
Starts an asynchronous run for `query` on the session and returns 202
immediately; follow progress via the events or stream endpoints. The
slash commands `/terminate` and `/status` are handled inline without
starting a run. A session executes one run at a time, so a second
call while one is active returns 409.

202.description: Run started; poll the events endpoint or open the stream.

400.description: The `query` field is missing, or the named project is invalid.

403.description: An explicit `cwd` was supplied but `api.allow_external_cwd` is off.

409.description: A run is already active for this session.

post.description:
Restart work on a failed or incomplete session. `retry` re-runs the last user query (optionally replaced via `edited_text`); `continue` resumes from where the run stopped. The run inherits the session's prior context. Wiki indexing sessions resume from their checkpoint and return a `job_id` to monitor instead of a `run_id`.
Restarts work on a failed or incomplete session. `retry` re-runs the
last user query, optionally edited via `edited_text`; `continue`
resumes from where the run stopped. The run inherits the session's
prior context and settings. Wiki indexing sessions resume from their
checkpoint instead and return a `job_id` to monitor rather than a
`run_id`.

202.description: Recovery run started; body carries `run_id` (or `job_id` for wiki jobs).

400.description: `action` is not `retry`/`continue`, or nothing to recover from.

409.description: A run is already active for this session.

post.description:
Mint a share token for the session. Anyone holding the token can read the transcript via GET /api/share/{token} without an API key.
Mints a share token for the session. Anyone holding the token can
read the transcript via GET /api/share/{token} without an API key.

200.description: Share record with the new token.

404.description: No session with that id exists.

get.description:
Open a Server-Sent Events stream. The stored backlog is replayed first, then new events are pushed as they happen (`data: <json>` frames). Heartbeat comments keep the connection alive and a terminal `stream_end` frame is sent when the run finishes. EventSource cannot set headers, so the key may be passed as the `api_key` query param.
Opens a Server-Sent Events stream. The stored backlog is replayed
first, then new events are pushed as they happen. Heartbeat comments
keep the connection alive, and a terminal `stream_end` frame is sent
when the run finishes. Because EventSource cannot set headers, the API
key may be passed as the `api_key` query parameter instead.

parameters.description: API key, for EventSource clients that cannot set the `X-API-Key` header.
parameters.name: api_key

patch.description:
Save a user-provided display title for the session. Titles are trimmed and capped at 120 characters. Use POST on the same path to have the model generate a title instead.
Saves a user-provided display title for the session. Titles are
trimmed and capped at 120 characters.

400.description: The `title` field is missing or empty.

404.description: No session with that id exists.

post.description:
Ask the configured model to produce a title from the transcript, save it, and append a `title_update` event to the session. Returns 422 when no usable title could be generated.
Asks the configured model to produce a title from the transcript,
saves it, and appends a `title_update` event to the session. Returns
422 when no usable title could be generated.

404.description: No session with that id exists.

422.description: No usable title could be generated.

get.description:
Return token usage split between the root agent and sub-agents, including peak and billed input figures plus compaction statistics. `total_input_tokens_billed` is the cost-accounting number; the peak fields describe context pressure.
Returns token usage split between the root agent and sub-agents,
including peak and billed input figures and compaction statistics.

Public endpoint. Return the shared session's transcript and summary for a valid token — no API key required; possession of the token is the only credential.
Public endpoint. Returns the shared session's transcript and summary
for a valid token. No API key is required; possession of the token is
the only credential.

404.description: No share token matches.

get.description:
List the available skills, including those contributed by installed plugins, with their descriptions, tool allowlists, and invocation flags. Pass `project` to include project-local skills. Activate a skill for a run via the `skill` field of a query.
Returns the available skills, including those contributed by installed
plugins, with their descriptions, tool allowlists, and invocation
flags. Activate a skill for a run via the `skill` field of a query.

parameters.description: Configured project name. Includes skills defined inside that project.
parameters.name: project

get.description:
List every known tool integration with its enablement state, the MCP server it comes from, and a `scope` of `global`, `project`, or `plugin`. Pass `project` to include tools configured inside that project. Use the `tool_id` values in a session's `mcp_tools` allowlist to scope what a run may call.
Returns every known tool integration with its enablement state, the
MCP server it comes from, and a `scope` of `global`, `project`, or
`plugin`. Pass `project` to include tools configured inside that
project. Use the `tool_id` values in a session's `mcp_tools` allowlist
to scope what a run may call.

parameters.description: Configured project name. Includes tools from that project's own MCP configuration.
parameters.name: project

post.description:
Register a server-managed project (as opposed to a static config one). When `path` is omitted the server provisions a folder. Use the returned `project_id` with the other `/api/v_projects` endpoints and as `managed:<project_id>` when creating sessions.
Registers a project managed by the server, as opposed to one defined
in static configuration. When `path` is omitted the server provisions
a folder for it. Use the returned `project_id` with the other
`/api/v_projects` endpoints, and as `managed:<project_id>` when
creating sessions.

400.description: The `name` field is missing or empty.

delete.description:
Remove a managed project record. Returns 204 with an empty body on success. Deleting a project that has worktrees removes only the project record itself.
Removes the managed project record. Returns 204 with an empty body on
success.

parameters.description: Managed project id returned by POST /api/v_projects.
parameters.name: project_id

404.description: No managed project with that id exists.

get.description:
Fetch a managed project's full record: filesystem path, worktree linkage (`is_worktree`, `parent_project_id`, `branch`), and timestamps. Only **managed** ids are accepted here; configured projects are listed via GET /api/projects.
Returns the full project record, including its filesystem path,
worktree linkage (`is_worktree`, `parent_project_id`, `branch`), and
timestamps. Only managed project ids are accepted here; configured
projects are listed via GET /api/projects.

parameters.description: Managed project id returned by POST /api/v_projects.
parameters.name: project_id

404.description: No managed project with that id exists.

patch.description:
Update a managed project's `name` and/or `description`. Fields omitted from the body are left unchanged. A project's path and worktree linkage are immutable after creation.
Updates the name and/or description. Fields omitted from the body are
left unchanged. The path and worktree linkage of a project cannot be
changed after creation.

parameters.description: Managed project id returned by POST /api/v_projects.
parameters.name: project_id

404.description: No managed project with that id exists.

get.description:
List `branches`, the `current_branch` (null when HEAD is detached), and `branches_in_use` — branches already checked out by the parent repo or another worktree, which `git worktree add` would refuse. When the path is missing or not a git repo the call still returns 200 with `git_repo` false and a `reason`.
Returns `branches`, the `current_branch` (null when HEAD is detached),
and `branches_in_use`, the branches already checked out by the parent
repository or another worktree. UIs should disable in-use entries,
since creating a worktree for them fails. When the project path is
missing or not a git repository the call still returns 200 with
`git_repo` false and a `reason`.

parameters.description: Managed project id, configured project name, or git identity such as `owner/repo` (any alias of the repository resolves).
parameters.name: project_id

200.description: Branch listing, or `git_repo: false` with a reason.

404.description: No project resolves from this id, name, or git identity.

409.description: A bare repo name matched multiple repositories; use host/owner/repo.

get.description:
List the union of worktrees created through this API and worktrees added on disk with plain git. Each entry has a `managed` flag — managed entries carry a `project_id` sessions can be pinned to — and a `clean` flag indicating it has no uncommitted changes.
Returns the union of worktrees created through this API and worktrees
added on disk with plain git. Each entry has a `managed` flag; managed
entries carry a `project_id` that sessions can be pinned to. Every
entry includes a `clean` flag indicating it has no uncommitted
changes.

parameters.description: Managed project id, configured project name, or git identity such as `owner/repo` (any alias of the repository resolves).
parameters.name: project_id

404.description: No project resolves from this id, name, or git identity.

409.description: A bare repo name matched multiple repositories; use host/owner/repo.

post.description:
Check out `branch` in a new worktree folder and register it as a child managed project. Pass `base` to create a fresh branch from that ref instead of requiring `branch` to exist. Configured projects are promoted to managed projects automatically. Worktree lifecycle is system-owned: a clean worktree is removed when its session ends.
Checks out `branch` in a new worktree folder and registers it as a
child managed project. Pass `base` to create a fresh branch from that
ref instead of requiring `branch` to exist. Configured projects are
promoted to managed projects automatically so the worktree gets a
stable parent. Worktree lifecycle is system owned: a clean worktree is
removed automatically when its session ends.

parameters.description: Managed project id, configured project name, or git identity such as `owner/repo` (any alias of the repository resolves).
parameters.name: project_id

400.description: The `branch` field is missing, or the project is not a git repository.

404.description: No project resolves from this id, name, or git identity.

409.description: The branch is already checked out elsewhere, or the worktree path exists.

delete.description:
Remove a managed worktree. Idempotent: deleting one that is already gone returns 200 with status `already_absent`. A worktree with uncommitted changes is refused with 409 unless `force=true`. Worktrees created outside this API must be removed with git directly.
Removes a managed worktree. The call is idempotent: deleting a
worktree that is already gone returns 200 with status
`already_absent`. A worktree with uncommitted changes is refused with
409 unless `force=true`. Worktrees created outside this API must be
removed with git directly.

parameters.description: Parent project: managed project id, configured project name, or git identity such as `owner/repo`.
parameters.name: project_id

parameters.description: The worktree's own `project_id` from the worktree listing.
parameters.name: worktree_id

parameters.description: Set to true to remove a worktree with uncommitted changes.
parameters.name: force

200.description: Worktree already absent; nothing to do.

400.description: The worktree does not belong to this project.

409.description: The worktree has uncommitted changes and `force` was not set.

post.description:
Stream a draft answer as **server-sent events** (`text/event-stream`). The backing session id is sent up front in the `X-Mewbo-Session` response header.

**Event frames:**

- `data: {"token": "<delta>"}` — one per LLM token delta, in order.
- `data: {"done": true, "session_id": "<id>"}` — terminal success frame; carries the backing session id so a streaming caller can resolve the trace.
- `data: {"error": "<reason>"}` — emitted instead of `done` on a mid-stream failure.

Pass an optional `workspace` (wiki slug) to add retrieval grounding before streaming starts, or `model` to override the configured default with any LiteLLM model id (a non-string value is ignored). Consume with an SSE client (e.g. `EventSource`), not a buffered JSON reader.
Streams a draft answer as server-sent events. Each token arrives as a
`data: {"token": "<delta>"}` frame, and the stream ends with a terminal
`data: {"done": true, "session_id": "<id>"}` frame; a mid-flight failure
emits a `data: {"error": "<reason>"}` frame instead. The backing session
id is also sent up front in the `X-Mewbo-Session` response header. A
workspace adds retrieval grounding before streaming starts. The optional
`model` field accepts any configured LiteLLM model id; a non-string
value is ignored.

200.description: Server-sent event stream of token frames (token / done / error); see description.

400.description: Malformed, missing, or invalid request body or parameters.

post.description:
Run a schema-constrained, tool-using structured query and get back a JSON object that validates against your `schema`.

**Two execution modes** (set via the `mode` field):

- **`agentic`** (default) — kicks off a session-backed, tool-using run and returns a **run handle** of the form `<session_id>:r<seq>`. The request waits a few seconds, so fast runs come back inline with `status:"completed"` and `output` attached; slower runs return `status:"running"` — poll `GET /v1/structured/{run_id}` until it completes, or attach to `GET /api/sessions/{session_id}/stream` using the part of the handle before the first `:`.
- **`synthesis`** — a no-loop, retrieval-only single round-trip (no tools). Returns **inline** with `status:"completed"`, the validated `output`, and grounding `citations` — lower latency for cheap structured extraction.

`workspace` grounds the run on a wiki slug or a search workspace (a mapped search workspace also grants graph traversal tools, making the agentic run graph-first). `model` overrides the configured default with any LiteLLM model id; a non-string value is ignored.

Example: `{"query": "List the public endpoints", "schema": {"type": "object", ...}, "mode": "synthesis"}`.
Starts an agentic run that answers the query with a JSON object validating
against the supplied schema. The response always carries a run handle of the
form `<session_id>:r<seq>`. The request waits briefly before returning, so
fast runs come back inline with status `completed` and the output attached.
Slower runs return status `running`: poll GET /v1/structured/{run_id}, or
attach to GET /api/sessions/{session_id}/stream using the part of the handle
before the first colon. The optional `model` field accepts any configured
LiteLLM model id; a non-string value is ignored. Set `mode` to `synthesis`
for a no-loop, retrieval-only single round-trip (no tools) that returns
inline with status `completed` plus grounding citations — lower latency for
cheap structured extraction.

200.description: Run handle (agentic) or inline result (synthesis); output attached when completed

400.description: Malformed, missing, or invalid request body or parameters.

409.description: The resource is in a conflicting state for this operation.

422.description: Understood but unprocessable — the request could not be carried out.

503.description: The feature is not configured or is temporarily unavailable.

get.description:
Resolve a run handle returned by `POST /v1/structured` to its current state. While the run is in flight the body is `{run_id, status}`. Once a validated output exists, `status` is `completed` and `output` carries the schema-validated object; graph-grounded runs also carry a `provenance` object summarizing the pathways routed and probes executed. A run that ends without a valid output returns 422 with an error envelope. Poll this endpoint until `status` is `completed` (or a 4xx).
Resolves a run handle to its current state. While the run is in flight the
body is `{run_id, status}`. Once a validated output exists the status is
`completed` and the output is attached; graph-grounded runs also carry a
`provenance` object summarizing the pathways routed and probes executed.
A run that ends without a valid output returns 422 with an error envelope.

parameters.description: Run handle returned by POST /v1/structured, in the form <session_id>:r<seq>.
parameters.name: run_id

200.description: Run status, with output and provenance once completed

404.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

422.description: Understood but unprocessable — the request could not be carried out.

503.description: The feature is not configured or is temporarily unavailable.

MaskError.description: When any error occurs on mask

ParseError.description: When a mask can't be parsed

tags.description: A session is the unit of conversation and audit. Create one, send it queries, steer it while it runs, and read everything back: events, sub-agent activity, token usage, diffs, and exports. Streams are push-based server-sent events. This is the surface the web console runs on.
tags.name: Sessions

tags.description: Projects point sessions at a working directory. Managed projects can be created over the API; git-backed projects additionally expose branches and isolated worktrees, so several sessions can work the same repository without stepping on each other.
tags.name: Projects & Worktrees

tags.description: Agentic Search over saved multi-source workspaces. Create a run against a workspace, then poll it or follow its event stream to a cited answer.
tags.name: Workspaces & Runs

tags.description: Source catalog and Source Capability Graph (SCG) indexing. Map jobs index a source's reachable schemas and pathways so search can route through them. Requires the SCG feature to be enabled.
tags.name: Source Graph

tags.description: Schema-constrained agentic runs. Send a query plus a JSON Schema and get back an object that validates against it, with optional workspace grounding and graph-first provenance. Pass `"mode": "synthesis"` for a no-loop, single round-trip variant that returns inline instead of a polling handle.
tags.name: Structured Outputs

tags.description: Token-by-token draft answers over server-sent events. The lowest latency surface; no schema, no tool use.
tags.name: Draft Streaming

tags.description: Discover which LLM models the configured proxy currently serves.
tags.name: Models

tags.description: Introspect the tool registry and available skills, optionally scoped to a project. Useful for building pickers and capability checks.
tags.name: Tools & Skills

tags.description: List installed plugins, browse configured marketplaces, and install or uninstall plugins. Plugin components load into new sessions.
tags.name: Plugins

tags.description: Read and dismiss the notification feed shown in the console.
tags.name: Notifications

tags.description: Read and patch the server configuration. Secrets are write-only and protected fields are never exposed; the schema endpoint describes exactly what a client may render and edit.
tags.name: Configuration

tags.description: Mint, list, and revoke API keys. Only the master token may mint. Give each integration its own revocable key.
tags.name: API Keys

tags.description: CI and VCS automation. Lets a pipeline hand issues and pull requests to Mewbo agents (see the CI Agent Pickup guide).
tags.name: Automation
